methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $400M - $2.0B.

data.narrative:
Zeno Power is a Series B-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $50M to date. Valuation analysis draws from a comparables universe of 5 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers, with direct same-sector, same-stage comparables including Cowboy Space Corporation ($2.0B), Aalyria ($1.3B), and Hubble Network ($400M).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $50M raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implies a $95M valuation. Peer-median analysis across the three same-stage Space & Aerospace peers yields a $1.3B median valuation, with a range spanning $400M to $2.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of disclosed revenue for the target company.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $81M (low) to $1.5B (high), with a median of $698M. The wide dispersion reflects material variance in private peer valuations within the sector. Key uncertainties include whether Zeno Power's technology differentiation and market position align closer to the lower-capitalized peers or tier-one operators like Cowboy Space, and the absence of revenue visibility prevents anchoring to public trading multiples.
